query: Belém Mission 1.5 initiative participating countries implementation details

{"query":"Belém Mission 1.5 initiative participating countries implementation details","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1648,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://unfccc.int/process-and-meetings/conferences/un-climate-change-conference-antalya-november-2026/belem-mission-to-15","title":"Belém Mission to 1.5 | UNFCCC","content":"The Belém Mission to 1.5 was conceived in the context of the Global Mutirão Decision, aimed at enabling ambition and implementation of nationally determined contributions (NDCs) and national adaptation plans (NAPs), to reflect on accelerating implementation, international cooperation and investment.
The Belém Mission to 1.5 will involve a series of activities and engagements throughout 2026 focused on identifying key opportunities to enable and accelerate the ambition and implementation of NDCs and NAPs, as well as reflecting on ways to enhance international cooperation and investments in those climate plans.
The Belém Mission to 1.5 will culminate in a report released ahead of COP 31, outlining key opportunities, barriers, and actionable solutions to keeping 1.5ºC within reach and strengthening adaptation and resilience in a changing world.
Submissions are welcome by 30 June 2026.
What action areas and solutions have the highest impact potential to support the submission, ambition and implementation of NDCs and NAPs.
- Remarks from the COP29, COP30 and COP31 Presidencies"},{"url":"https://www.globalcovenantofmayors.org/press/cop30-how-local-governments-drove-climate-ambition-in-belem/","title":"COP30: How Local Governments Drove Climate Ambition in Belém - Global Covenant of Mayors","content":"To kick off subnationals’ position and efforts, during the COP30 World Leaders Summit, a delegation of local leaders – including Anne Hidalgo, Mayor of Paris and GCoM Global Ambassador; Katrin Jammeh, Mayor of Malmö and President of ICLEI; Igor Normando, Mayor of Belém; and Helder Barbalho, Governor of Pará – formally presented the Local Leaders Forum joint outcome statement to UN Secretary-General António Guterres.
Among the subnational delegates, GCoM Regional Ambassador Mayor Mohamed Sefiani of Chefchaouen, Morocco, participated in the meaningful discussions, in the critical second week of the COP.
GCoM Regional Ambassador Mayor Mohamed Sefiani of Chefchaouen, Morocco, alongside other city leaders from Maringá, Bacarena, Benevides, and Governor Helder Barbalho (Pará) presented the COP30 Local Leaders Forum Outcome Statement directly to President Lula and Minister of Cities Jader Filho.
Its core function is to provide targeted support to nations as they execute their Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs) and National Adaptation Plans (NAPs).
The Belém Mission to 1.5: An action-focused initiative managed by the COP29-COP31 troika."},{"url":"https://news.un.org/en/story/2025/11/1166433","title":"Belém COP30 delivers climate finance boost and a pledge to plan fossil fuel transition | UN News","content":"Adaptation boost: Double adaptation finance by 2025 and triple by 2035.
More than 80 countries backed Brazil’s proposal for a formal ‘roadmap.’
Negotiators worked through the night on Friday – to bridge gaps on finance and ambition, with Brazil’s presidency steering discussions toward a politically workable outcome focused on support and implementation of agreements from past COPs.
Tropical Forests Forever Fund: Raised $5.5 billion and now includes 53 participating countries; at least 20 per cent of resources go directly to Indigenous Peoples and local communities."},{"url":"https://www.c2es.org/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/Considerations-and-recommendations-for-the-Belem-Mission-to-1_5.pdf","title":"Considerations and Recommendations for the Belém ...","date":"5 days ago","content":"5 days ago — Guided by the UAE, Azerbaijan, and Brazil Presidencies (the COP Presidencies Troika), from 2024-. 2025, Mission 1.5 consisted of a series of ...Read more"},{"url":"https://unfccc.int/sites/default/files/resource/Submission_C2ES.pdf","title":"Considerations and Recommendations for the Belém ...","date":"4 days ago","content":"4 days ago — Launched in November 2025, the Belém Mission to 1.5 is aimed at enabling ambition and implementation of nationally determined contributions … 7 ...8 pages"},{"url":"https://www.c2es.org/document/considerations-and-recommendations-for-the-belem-mission-to-1-5/","title":"Considerations and Recommendations for the Belém ...","date":"5 days ago","content":"5 days ago — Launched in November 2025, the Belém Mission to 1.5 is aimed at enabling ambition and implementation of nationally determined contributions ...Read more"}]}

tavily.raw: {"query":"Belém Mission 1.5 initiative participating countries implementation details","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://unfccc.int/process-and-meetings/conferences/un-climate-change-conference-antalya-november-2026/belem-mission-to-15","title":"Belém Mission to 1.5","content":"The Belém Mission to 1.5 ・ aimed at enabling ambition and implementation of nationally determined contributions ・ we – Türkiye, Australia, Brazil and","score":0.7318455,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.c2es.org/wp-content/uploads/2026/07/Considerations-and-recommendations-for-the-Belem-Mission-to-1_5.pdf","title":"Considerations and Recommendations for the Belém ...","content":"Guided by the UAE, Azerbaijan, and Brazil Presidencies (the COP Presidencies Troika), from 2024-. 2025, Mission 1.5 consisted of a series of","score":0.6342494,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://news.un.org/en/story/2025/11/1166433","title":"Belém COP30 delivers climate finance boost and a pledge to plan fossil fuel transition | UN News","content":"# Belém COP30 delivers climate finance boost and a pledge to plan fossil fuel transition. Climate activists hold protest signs at the COP30 conference in Belém, Brazil. In a pivotal outcome at COP30 in Belém, Brazil, countries agreed on a sweeping package to scale up climate finance and accelerate implementation of the Paris Agreement – but without a clear commitment to move away from fossil fuels. That was the message from UN climate chief Simon Stiell as COP30 wrapped up following marathon talks on Friday night which stretched into sunrise Saturday – signaling a turning point for climate ambition and global solidarity. * **New initiatives**: Launch of the Global Implementation Accelerator and Belém Mission to 1.5°C to drive ambition and implementation. The burning of fossil fuels emits greenhouse gases that are by far the largest contributors to global warming, making this omission a point of concern for many nations, including negotiators from South America and the EU, as well as civil society groups.","score":0.57952,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://enb.iisd.org/belem-un-climate-change-conference-cop30-summary","title":"Summary report 10–21 November 2025","content":"The Brazilian Presidency managed to successfully launch substantive negotiations on the understanding that it would hold Presidency consultations on four of these items: implementing developed countries’ finance obligations under Paris Agreement Article 9.1; unilateral trade-restrictive measures; and responding to the latest NDCs and biennial transparency reports to address the 1.5°C ambition and implementation gaps. Pointing out that fossil fuels are by far the largest driver of climate change, Colombia questioned what message the Conference is sending to the world, underscoring that the “COP of truth cannot support an outcome that ignores science.” Panama, Uruguay, and several others in turn denounced the gavelling of the decision on the Global Goal on Adaptation (GGA) in spite of their delegations having raised their flags and signalling points of order, which were not acknowledged by the dais. Parties also: operationalized the Technology Implementation Programme; launched new work on finance flow alignment (Paris Agreement Article 2.1(c); updated the type of information to consider in *ex ante* climate finance reporting (Paris Agreement Article 9.5); concluded the review of the Warsaw International Mechanism on loss and damage; and agreed on activities aimed to support developing countries’ reporting, among others.","score":0.4977301,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://ecoltdgroup.com/blog-cop30-in-belem-a-pragmatic-step-forward-in-a-fractured-world","title":"Blog | COP30 in Belém: A pragmatic step forward in ... - E Co Ltd","content":"The Belém Mission to 1.5, an action-oriented platform under the COP29–COP31 troika (Azerbaijan–Brazil–Türkiye), aims to sustain political focus and foster","score":0.4974371,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":1.23,"request_id":"1b7bdca7-ebb6-4552-b924-9a2c1afabe28"}
